title: Bitter Crunchy Ukrainian Beet Pierogi
description: A classic Ukrainian dish featuring a tangy filling of beets, vanilla bean paste, and cure, all wrapped in a crispy, crunchy dough. Perfect as a side dish or snack.
instructions: Combine the flour and salt in a large mixing bowl.
Gradually add the water and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a thin circle.
Mash the beets in a bowl until they are well broken down.
Mix the vanilla bean paste and cure into the mashed beets.
Place a tablespoon of the beet filling in the center of each dough circle.
Spoon a small amount of water along the edge of the dough circle, then fold the dough over the filling to form a half-circle.
Press the edges of the dough together to seal the pierogi.
Repeat the process with the remaining dough and filling.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pierogi for 5-7 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
Serve the pierogi hot with sour cream or applesauce.
Makes 8-10 pierogi. Prep Time: 30 minutes. Cook Time: 15 minutes. Total Time: 45 minutes. Servings: 8-10.
